Option D is incorrect because the colon incorrectly interrupts the prepositional phrase "such as," which already functions to introduce the list; no colon should appear between "such as" and its objects.
subject: ELA

question: Which sentences correctly use a colon to introduce a list or a quotation? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: The documentary explores three pressing environmental issues: deforestation, ocean acidification, and biodiversity loss.
answer_options.B: The mayor concluded her speech with a powerful reminder: "Democracy requires active participation from every citizen."
answer_options.C: The factors contributing to the decline include: habitat destruction, pollution, and invasive species.
answer_options.D: She argued that: the proposed solution would create more problems than it solved.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the colon follows a complete independent clause ("The documentary explores three pressing environmental issues") to introduce a list. Option B is correct because the colon follows an independent clause ("The mayor concluded her speech with a powerful reminder") to introduce a direct quotation. Option C is incorrect because the colon separates the transitive verb "include" from its objects, leaving an incomplete clause before the colon. Option D is incorrect because the colon follows the subordinating conjunction "that," which splits the verb "argued" from its complement clause and prevents the preceding text from standing as a complete independent clause.
subject: ELA

question: In which sentences is the bolded word spelled correctly? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The scientist carefully documented every **occurrence** of the phenomenon.
answer_options.B: Please keep these documents in a **seperate** folder.
answer_options.C: The principal emphasized the importance of **attendance**.
answer_options.D: The **committee** will make its decision tomorrow.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'occurrence' is spelled correctly with double 'c' and double 'r' (formed from 'occur' plus the suffix '-ence,' which requires doubling the final 'r'). Option C is correct because 'attendance' is spelled correctly with double 't' (retaining the double 't' from 'attend'). Option D is correct because 'committee' is spelled correctly with double 'm,' double 't,' and double 'e.' Option B is incorrect because 'seperate' should be spelled 'separate' (the vowel after 'p' is 'a,' not 'e').
subject: ELA

question: Select ALL that apply: Which of the following sentences contain no spelling errors?

answer_options.A: The conscientious editor refused to accommodate the unnecessary changes.
answer_options.B: The occurence of such events is rare in this region.
answer_options.C: It is necessary to maintain separate records for each department.
answer_options.D: The committee will definately review the proposal tomorrow.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because all words—including the commonly misspelled 'conscientious,' 'accommodate,' and 'unnecessary'—are spelled correctly. Option C is correct because 'necessary' and 'separate' are spelled correctly. Option B is incorrect because 'occurence' should be spelled 'occurrence' (with double 'r'). Option D is incorrect because 'definately' should be spelled 'definitely' (ending in 'ite' rather than 'ate').
subject: ELA

question: A student is formatting a research paper according to the MLA Handbook (9th edition). Which of the following formatting decisions conform to MLA guidelines? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Set one-inch margins on the top, bottom, left, and right of every page.
answer_options.B: Include a header in the upper right corner with your last name and the page number, 0.5 inch from the top.
answer_options.C: Double-space the entire document, including the Works Cited page and any block quotations.
answer_options.D: Type the title in 14-point bold font to distinguish it from the body text.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the MLA Handbook requires one-inch margins on all sides of each page. Option B is correct because MLA guidelines specify placing your last name and the page number in the upper right corner, a half-inch from the top and flush with the right margin. Option C is correct because MLA requires double-spacing throughout the entire paper, including block quotations of more than four lines and Works Cited entries, with no extra spaces between paragraphs or entries. Option D is incorrect because MLA requires the title to be in the same 12-point font as the rest of the paper (typically Times New Roman), centered, and without bold, italics, underlining, or quotation marks.
subject: ELA

question: A student is revising a research paper draft to ensure it conforms to MLA 9th edition guidelines. The draft currently has 1.5-inch margins on all sides, uses single spacing for the Works Cited list, and lacks paragraph indentation. Which revisions are necessary to meet MLA formatting standards? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Adjust the margins to 1 inch on all sides.
answer_options.B: Apply double-spacing throughout the Works Cited list.
answer_options.C: Indent the first line of each paragraph by one-half inch.
answer_options.D: Insert a header with the page number centered at the bottom of each page.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the MLA Handbook (9th edition) requires 1-inch margins on all sides (top, bottom, left, and right), so the student must change the 1.5-inch margins. Option B is correct because MLA guidelines mandate double-spacing throughout the entire paper, including the Works Cited page, so single-spacing must be corrected. Option C is correct because MLA style requires the first line of each paragraph to be indented one-half inch (0.5 inch) from the left margin. Option D is incorrect because MLA format requires a running header in the upper right corner (one-half inch from the top) containing the author's last name and the page number, not a page number centered at the bottom.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the sentence below and use the context clues to determine the meaning of the bolded word.

"After the severe drought, the farmer's harvest was **meager**, providing barely enough grain for the family to survive the winter."

Which of the following meanings are supported by the context? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: abundant
answer_options.B: scarce
answer_options.C: plentiful
answer_options.D: inadequate

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because 'scarce' matches the context clue 'barely enough grain,' which indicates a shortage or limited supply. Option D is correct because 'inadequate' fits the context of the harvest being insufficient to meet the family's needs for survival. Option A is incorrect because 'abundant' means plentiful or more than enough, which contradicts the context of scarcity described. Option C is incorrect because 'plentiful' also means existing in large amounts, which is the opposite of the limited harvest described in the sentence.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the sentence and use context clues to determine the meaning of the underlined word. Select ALL that apply.

"The professor's lecture was so ___esoteric___ that only the three graduate students in the front row nodded in understanding, while the undergraduates checked their phones in confusion."

answer_options.A: Intended for or understood by only a small number of people
answer_options.B: Deliberately confusing and misleading
answer_options.C: Highly specialized or technical
answer_options.D: Widely accessible and easy to comprehend

answer_explanation: The context indicates that only a specific group (graduate students) understood the lecture, while others (undergraduates) did not, suggesting the content was exclusive and advanced. Therefore, A is correct because 'esoteric' means intended for a select few. C is correct because the contrast between graduate and undergraduate comprehension implies the material was specialized or technical. B is incorrect because 'esoteric' does not imply deception or intentional misleading; it refers to the accessibility of knowledge, not its honesty. D is incorrect because it describes the antonym of 'esoteric'; the context explicitly shows the lecture was not easily understood by everyone present.
subject: ELA

question: Which sentences correctly use word forms that indicate different parts of speech? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: The committee's decision to postpone the meeting was met with widespread disappointment.
answer_options.B: The scientist's assumption about the chemical reaction proved to be accurate after further testing.
answer_options.C: The novel contains a subtle allude to the author's childhood experiences.
answer_options.D: The students were asked to analysis the poem's symbolism before class.

answer_explanation: Option A correctly uses 'decision,' the noun form of the verb 'decide,' formed by dropping the final '-e' and adding '-sion.' It functions correctly as the subject of the sentence. Option B correctly uses 'assumption,' the noun form of the verb 'assume,' formed by adding '-ion' with a stem change (assume → assumpt + ion). It serves correctly as the subject of the clause. Option C incorrectly uses the verb 'allude' where the noun 'allusion' (formed by replacing '-de' with '-sion') is required after the article 'a' and adjective 'subtle.' Option D incorrectly uses the noun 'analysis' where the verb 'analyze' is required after the infinitive marker 'to.'
subject: ELA

question: Which word pairs correctly demonstrate a suffix change that converts the base word into a different part of speech? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: decide — decision
answer_options.B: assume — assumption
answer_options.C: accident — accidental
answer_options.D: book — booklet

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because "decision" is the noun form of the verb "decide," formed by replacing the final -de with the suffix -sion, shifting the word from a verb (to decide) to a noun (the act of deciding). Option B is correct because "assumption" is the noun form of the verb "assume," formed by dropping the final -e and adding the suffix -tion (with orthographic assimilation producing the stem assump-), converting the verb (to assume) into a noun (the act of assuming). Option C is correct because "accidental" is the adjective form of the noun "accident," formed by adding the suffix -al, which changes the word from a noun (an event) to an adjective (describing something happening by chance). Option D is incorrect because both "book" and "booklet" are nouns; while the suffix -let changes the meaning to indicate a smaller version, it does not change the part of speech from one grammatical category to another.
subject: ELA

question: While reading a poetry analysis, you encounter the word "ephemeral" and want to determine its precise meaning and its part of speech. Which of the following reference sources would provide this information? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: A dictionary entry labeling "ephemeral" as an adjective and defining it as "lasting for a very short time"
answer_options.B: A thesaurus entry for "permanent" listing antonyms such as "temporary, fleeting, ephemeral"
answer_options.C: An encyclopedia article about the life cycles of various insect species
answer_options.D: A literary glossary identifying "ephemeral" as an adjective meaning "short-lived or transient"

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because a standard dictionary entry provides both the precise definition of a word and its part of speech. Option D is correct because a specialized glossary offers specific definitions and grammatical labels for terms within a particular field. Option B is incorrect because a thesaurus entry for a different word lists semantic relationships but does not provide the precise definition or part of speech for "ephemeral" itself. Option C is incorrect because an encyclopedia article provides general factual information about a topic rather than lexical information such as word definitions or parts of speech.
subject: ELA

question: While analyzing a poem, you encounter the unfamiliar word "ephemeral." You need to determine its precise meaning, its pronunciation, and its etymology for your literary analysis. Which of the following strategies using reference materials would effectively help you obtain at least one of these specific details? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Look up "ephemeral" in a collegiate dictionary and examine the phonetic spelling, definition, and etymology note.
answer_options.B: Consult a thesaurus to compare synonyms such as "transient," "fleeting," and "evanescent" to clarify the precise meaning.
answer_options.C: Check a style manual to verify the standard spelling and capitalization rules for "ephemeral."
answer_options.D: Use an etymological dictionary to trace the word's history and linguistic roots.

answer_explanation: A collegiate dictionary (A) provides the pronunciation via phonetic spelling, the precise definition with part of speech, and typically includes an etymology note indicating the word's origin, thereby supplying multiple required details. A thesaurus (B) lists synonyms and antonyms, which help clarify the precise meaning by revealing nuanced differences between similar words. An etymological dictionary (D) specializes in tracing word origins and historical development, directly fulfilling the etymology requirement. A style manual (C) focuses on formatting, spelling conventions, and grammar rules for writing, but it does not provide pronunciation, etymology, or the type of precise meaning clarification described in the standard.
subject: ELA

question: While reading a science article, a student encounters the sentence: "The resilient material bounced back to its original shape after being compressed." The student initially believes that "resilient" means "able to spring back into shape after being bent or pressed." Which of the following strategies would help the student verify this preliminary determination? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Consult a standard dictionary to confirm the definition of "resilient."
answer_options.B: Reread the surrounding sentences to check if "able to spring back into shape after being bent or pressed" fits the context.
answer_options.C: Replace "resilient" with "fragile" to test if the sentence conveys the opposite meaning.
answer_options.D: Break the word into syllables and guess the meaning based on the prefix "re-" alone.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because consulting a standard dictionary is an explicit method for verifying a word's meaning, as described in the standard. Option B is correct because rereading the surrounding text allows the student to check whether the inferred meaning aligns with the evidence in the passage (the material "bounced back to its original shape"). Option C is incorrect because testing an antonym does not verify the preliminary meaning of the word itself; it only checks for opposition. Option D is incorrect because guessing based on word parts without external verification does not confirm the preliminary determination; it merely creates another unverified inference.
subject: ELA

question: After encountering the sentence, "Despite the treacherous weather and dwindling supplies, the expedition leader remained sanguine about completing the trek," a student determines that the word sanguine means "hopeful and confident despite difficulties." Which of the following strategies would best help the student verify this preliminary determination? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Consult a reputable dictionary to confirm whether definitions of "sanguine" include "hopeful" or "confident"
answer_options.B: Substitute "hopeful" for "sanguine" in the sentence to check if the sentence remains logical and coherent
answer_options.C: Assume the word means "bloodthirsty" because it shares the Latin root "sang-" (blood) with "sanguinary," without consulting any other sources
answer_options.D: Re-read the surrounding paragraph for additional context clues about the leader's attitude toward the challenging circumstances

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because consulting a dictionary provides an authoritative definition to verify whether "sanguine" actually conveys the meaning of hopefulness or confidence. Option B is correct because substituting the inferred synonym into the original context tests whether the preliminary determination fits logically within the sentence's meaning. Option D is correct because examining surrounding text for consistent context clues offers corroborating evidence to support the inferred meaning. Option C is incorrect because relying solely on a shared root without checking the actual definition leads to an erroneous conclusion ("bloodthirsty" contradicts the context) and fails to verify the preliminary determination of "hopeful."
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentence from a principal's letter to parents: "The school district is implementing 'right-sizing measures' to address enrollment declines, which has created a 'happy sadness' among students who will miss their old classrooms but appreciate the new resources." Which statements correctly interpret the figures of speech and their roles in the text? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: 'Right-sizing measures' is a euphemism that makes budget cuts and potential consolidation sound like a positive, necessary adjustment.
answer_options.B: 'Happy sadness' is an oxymoron that captures the mixed emotions students feel about leaving familiar spaces while gaining new resources.
answer_options.C: 'Right-sizing measures' is an oxymoron that combines opposite ideas about growth and reduction to confuse parents about the changes.
answer_options.D: 'Happy sadness' is literal language describing students who are physically smiling and crying at the same time during the move.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'right-sizing measures' is a euphemism that replaces harsh terms like 'budget cuts' or 'school closures' with business-friendly language to make the changes sound more palatable and logical. Option B is correct because 'happy sadness' is an oxymoron that joins contradictory emotions to emphasize the complex, bittersweet nature of the students' feelings about the transition. Option C is incorrect because 'right-sizing measures' is not an oxymoron; it does not combine opposite words but rather uses mild language to mask a difficult reality. Option D is incorrect because 'happy sadness' is figurative language meant to convey emotional complexity, not a literal description of facial expressions.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the excerpt from a political press briefing:

During the televised briefing, the press secretary announced that the military operation had resulted in 'collateral damage' rather than civilian casualties. He described the tense diplomatic standoff as a 'peaceful confrontation' between the rival nations. The room fell into a 'deafening silence' as journalists processed the implications.

Which statements correctly interpret the figures of speech in this excerpt and analyze their roles in the text? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The phrase 'collateral damage' functions as a euphemism that softens the harsh reality of civilian casualties to make the military action more palatable to the public.
answer_options.B: The term 'peaceful confrontation' operates as an oxymoron that captures the contradictory nature of hostile parties engaging in non-violent negotiation.
answer_options.C: The expression 'deafening silence' serves as an oxymoron that figuratively conveys the overwhelming weight of the journalists' unspoken shock.
answer_options.D: The phrase 'collateral damage' is an example of personification that attributes human carelessness to the weapons used in the operation.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'collateral damage' is a euphemism that replaces the blunt term 'civilian casualties' with a detached, technical phrase, thereby minimizing the perceived severity of the harm and maintaining public support. Option B is correct because 'peaceful confrontation' combines contradictory terms—'peaceful' suggesting calm and 'confrontation' suggesting conflict—to form an oxymoron that emphasizes the tense paradox of diplomatic hostility without violence. Option C is correct because 'deafening silence' juxtaposes 'deafening' (extremely loud) with 'silence' (absence of sound) to create an oxymoron that figuratively expresses the heavy, oppressive quiet filled with unspoken tension. Option D is incorrect because 'collateral damage' does not attribute human characteristics to weapons or destruction; it is a euphemistic abstraction that obscures the human cost rather than personifying the instruments of war.
subject: ELA

question: Each sentence below uses a word that means 'careful about saving money and not wasting resources.' Which sentences use a word with a NEGATIVE connotation suggesting selfishness or excessive frugality? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Maria is thrifty, always clipping coupons and finding the best deals to support her family.
answer_options.B: The stingy landlord refused to repair the heating system even when the temperature dropped below freezing.
answer_options.C: By being economical with their supplies, the research team completed the project under budget.
answer_options.D: The miserly collector kept his rare coins locked away, never sharing them with the museum or even his own children.

answer_explanation: Options B and D are correct because they use words with negative connotations. 'Stingy' (Option B) suggests an ungenerous, selfish refusal to spend money even when necessary, carrying a judgmental tone. 'Miserly' (Option D) implies an excessive, pathological hoarding of wealth and an inability to share, evoking greed. Options A and C are incorrect because they use words with positive connotations. 'Thrifty' (Option A) suggests wise, prudent financial management for beneficial purposes. 'Economical' (Option C) implies efficient, intelligent use of resources without any suggestion of moral failing. All four words share the denotation of being careful with money, but 'stingy' and 'miserly' carry the specific negative nuance of selfishness required by the question.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences describing Clara's work as an editor. Which sentences use a word that carries a POSITIVE connotation of careful attention to detail? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Clara was meticulous, checking every comma and citation to ensure absolute accuracy in the final manuscript.
answer_options.B: Her scrupulous adherence to ethical guidelines earned the trust of all her clients and colleagues.
answer_options.C: The punctilious formatting requirements she imposed frustrated writers who found them unnecessarily rigid and time-consuming.
answer_options.D: Some authors found her fastidious editing style exhausting, as she rejected drafts for the smallest inconsistencies.

answer_explanation: Options A and B are correct because 'meticulous' and 'scrupulous' both denote careful attention to detail but carry positive connotations. In sentence A, 'meticulous' suggests thoroughness and precision valued in editing. In sentence B, 'scrupulous' implies moral integrity and diligent exactness, reinforced by the context of earning trust. Options C and D are incorrect because, although 'punctilious' and 'fastidious' similarly denote strict attention to detail, they carry negative connotations in these contexts. 'Punctilious' in sentence C implies excessive concern with trivial formalities that frustrates others, while 'fastidious' in sentence D suggests an overly critical, hard-to-please attitude that exhausts authors.
subject: ELA

question: The academic verb “corroborate” means “to confirm or give support to a statement, theory, or finding with evidence.” Which of the following sentences use this word accurately? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The security camera footage corroborates the alibi provided by the defendant's brother.
answer_options.B: The two historians decided to corroborate on writing the textbook chapter about ancient Rome.
answer_options.C: Several independent studies corroborate the theory that regular exercise improves cognitive function.
answer_options.D: The lawyer's argument was very corroborate and convinced the judge to dismiss the case.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it uses “corroborates” as a transitive verb showing that the footage (evidence) confirms the alibi (statement). Option C is correct because it uses “corroborate” to indicate that the studies (evidence) support the theory (finding). Option B is incorrect because it confuses “corroborate” with “collaborate”; corroborate does not mean to work jointly on a project. Option D is incorrect because “corroborate” is a verb, not an adjective; the sentence mistakenly uses it as an adjective instead of the past participle “corroborated” or a different descriptive word.
subject: ELA

question: Which of the following student actions best demonstrate the acquisition and accurate use of general academic and domain-specific vocabulary at the college and career readiness level, including independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: While researching for a history essay, Leo encounters the term 'hegemony.' He analyzes the Greek root 'hegemon' (leader), checks multiple academic sources to verify the nuanced meaning of dominance or leadership, and then accurately employs the term in his thesis statement to describe cultural influence.
answer_options.B: During a biology lab, Sofia comes across the word 'photosynthesis' and immediately asks the teacher to explain it without first reviewing the textbook diagrams or attempting to break down the word parts ('photo' meaning light and 'synthesis' meaning putting together).
answer_options.C: Reading a literary analysis, Jamal finds the phrase 'ubiquitous presence.' He uses context clues from the surrounding sentences to infer it means appearing everywhere, confirms this understanding by consulting an academic dictionary, and then uses the term correctly in his own analytical essay to describe a recurring symbol in the novel.
answer_options.D: Taylor highlights the domain-specific phrase 'opportunity cost' from an economics article and copies the definition 'the loss of potential gain from other alternatives' into a vocabulary list, but never attempts to apply the term to explain trade-offs in her own economic analysis presentation.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Leo demonstrates independence by analyzing Greek roots and consulting multiple sources to gather nuanced vocabulary knowledge, then accurately applies the domain-specific term in his writing. Option C is correct because Jamal independently uses context clues and reference materials to acquire the academic vocabulary, then demonstrates accurate comprehension by applying it correctly in his own analytical writing. Option B is incorrect because immediately asking the teacher without first attempting independent strategies (such as using word parts or context) shows a lack of independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge. Option D is incorrect because merely copying a definition without attempting to use or apply the term in speaking or writing does not constitute accurate acquisition or demonstrate the ability to use the vocabulary at the college and career readiness level.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the excerpt from a health education report, then answer the question.

Excerpt:
"Research published in the Journal of Adolescent Health indicates that teenagers experience a biological shift in their circadian rhythms during puberty, causing them to feel sleepy later at night and struggle with early morning wakefulness. When high schools start before 8:00 a.m., many students must wake up during deep sleep phases, leading to chronic sleep deprivation that affects concentration and mood. A comprehensive study conducted across three diverse districts found that schools with 8:30 a.m. start times reported 20% fewer tardiness incidents and significantly higher test scores in first-period classes compared to those starting at 7:30 a.m. Additionally, students at these schools visited the school nurse 15% less frequently for fatigue-related complaints and reported feeling less stressed throughout the day. Dr. Maria Chen, a pediatric sleep specialist, emphasizes that allowing adolescents to sleep according to their natural biological clocks improves both academic performance and mental health outcomes. However, changing start times requires adjusting bus schedules and after-school activity timelines, which many districts find logistically challenging to implement despite the clear health benefits."

Which sentences from the excerpt provide strong textual evidence that later school start times benefit student health and academic performance? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: A comprehensive study conducted across three diverse districts found that schools with 8:30 a.m. start times reported 20% fewer tardiness incidents and significantly higher test scores in first-period classes compared to those starting at 7:30 a.m.
answer_options.B: Additionally, students at these schools visited the school nurse 15% less frequently for fatigue-related complaints and reported feeling less stressed throughout the day.
answer_options.C: Dr. Maria Chen, a pediatric sleep specialist, emphasizes that allowing adolescents to sleep according to their natural biological clocks improves both academic performance and mental health outcomes.
answer_options.D: However, changing start times requires adjusting bus schedules and after-school activity timelines, which many districts find logistically challenging to implement despite the clear health benefits.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it provides specific statistical evidence linking later start times to improved academic outcomes (fewer tardies and higher test scores). Option B is correct because it offers concrete data regarding health benefits, specifically reduced nurse visits for fatigue and decreased stress levels. Option C is correct because it cites an expert authority directly stating that aligning school schedules with biological sleep patterns enhances both academic performance and mental health. Option D is incorrect because it discusses logistical obstacles to implementation rather than providing evidence of benefits to students; it actually acknowledges the benefits but focuses on why districts struggle to adopt the change.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the excerpt and the student analysis below. Select ALL sentences from the excerpt that provide strong and thorough textual evidence to support the analysis.

Excerpt:
Researchers at the Metropolitan Education Institute have documented significant correlations between sustained classroom noise levels and measurable declines in student academic performance across diverse urban environments. A three-year longitudinal study tracking 2,400 students across twelve urban middle and high schools found that learners in classrooms facing busy streets consistently scored an average of 12 percent lower on standardized reading comprehension tests compared to peers in quieter interior rooms. 'The constant sirens and traffic rumble make it impossible to concentrate during silent reading,' explains ninth-grader Marcus Chen, whose classroom overlooks a major intersection near the downtown business district, describing the daily disruption he faces. Acoustic engineers carefully measured sound levels exceeding 65 decibels—equivalent to a loud conversation—in these exterior classrooms during 40 percent of instructional time. While the district has successfully installed sound-dampening windows in two pilot schools, budget constraints have prevented wider implementation of these costly improvements. Furthermore, the comprehensive study revealed that teachers working in chronically high-noise environments spent 20 percent more time managing student behavior than their colleagues in quieter settings, significantly reducing the instructional minutes available for actual learning and content delivery.

Analysis: Exposure to urban noise pollution hinders student learning and disrupts classroom dynamics.

answer_options.A: A three-year longitudinal study tracking 2,400 students across twelve urban middle and high schools found that learners in classrooms facing busy streets consistently scored an average of 12 percent lower on standardized reading comprehension tests compared to peers in quieter interior rooms.
answer_options.B: 'The constant sirens and traffic rumble make it impossible to concentrate during silent reading,' explains ninth-grader Marcus Chen, whose classroom overlooks a major intersection near the downtown business district, describing the daily disruption he faces.
answer_options.C: Furthermore, the comprehensive study revealed that teachers working in chronically high-noise environments spent 20 percent more time managing student behavior than their colleagues in quieter settings, significantly reducing the instructional minutes available for actual learning and content delivery.
answer_options.D: While the district has successfully installed sound-dampening windows in two pilot schools, budget constraints have prevented wider implementation of these costly improvements.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it provides explicit statistical evidence directly linking noise exposure to reduced academic performance on standardized tests. Option B is correct because it offers qualitative testimony demonstrating how noise interferes with concentration during learning activities, supporting the inference that learning is hindered. Option C is correct because it presents data showing increased time spent on behavior management, which explicitly demonstrates disruption to classroom dynamics and loss of instructional time. Option D is incorrect because it discusses budgetary and logistical barriers to implementing solutions, which does not provide evidence regarding the negative effects of noise on student learning or classroom dynamics.
subject: ELA

question: Which of the following scenarios demonstrate a student meeting the expectations of CCSS.ELA-LITERACY.RI.9-10.10 for reading literary nonfiction? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: A ninth-grade student reads a grade-10 historical speech (high end of the 9–10 band), uses a teacher-provided glossary for difficult terms, and accurately identifies the author’s central argument.
answer_options.B: A tenth-grade student independently reads a complex grade-10 investigative journalism article and writes a coherent summary of the evidence presented without requiring teacher explanation.
answer_options.C: A ninth-grade student is unable to comprehend a grade-9 editorial until the teacher reads each paragraph aloud and restates the main ideas in simpler language.
answer_options.D: A tenth-grade student chooses to build confidence by reading grade-6 level news articles instead of texts within the grades 9–10 complexity band.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it depicts a ninth-grader reading material at the high end of the 9–10 text complexity band with appropriate scaffolding (a glossary) and demonstrating comprehension, which matches the grade-9 expectation. Option B is correct because it shows a tenth-grader independently reading and comprehending material at the high end of the 9–10 band, fulfilling the grade-10 expectation. Option C is incorrect because the student requires extensive, line-by-line teacher support to understand grade-level text, indicating a lack of the proficiency required by the standard. Option D is incorrect because the student is reading material well below the grades 9–10 text complexity band, which does not demonstrate the proficiency required by the standard.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the following excerpt from an essay on urbanization.

"Modern urban life operates on a rhythm fundamentally alien to the biological cadence of our ancestors. Where once we measured days by the arc of the sun and seasons by the migration of birds, we now navigate by the artificial glow of streetlights and the mechanical precision of transit schedules. This temporal displacement is not merely a matter of convenience but a profound cognitive shift. Studies suggest that constant exposure to artificial lighting disrupts circadian rhythms, yet we persist in this arrangement because the city promises something that nature cannot: the illusion of control. We trade the unpredictable variability of weather for climate-controlled interiors, the irregular bounty of harvests for the predictable abundance of supermarkets. Yet in this exchange, we risk becoming what the architect Louis Mumford once called 'mechanized barbarians'—creatures possessing sophisticated technology but suffering from sensory deprivation, surrounded by concrete yet starving for organic texture."

Based on the passage, which statements are supported by the text? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: The author believes that urban environments create a false sense of control over natural forces.
answer_options.B: The author suggests that modern city dwellers experience a disruption in natural biological patterns.
answer_options.C: The author argues that supermarkets offer less abundance than traditional harvests.
answer_options.D: The author implies that living in cities leads to a deficiency in sensory experiences.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text explicitly states that the city promises 'the illusion of control' that nature cannot provide. Option B is correct because the author describes urban life as operating on a rhythm 'alien to the biological cadence of our ancestors' and notes that artificial lighting 'disrupts circadian rhythms.' Option D is correct because the author describes modern dwellers as 'suffering from sensory deprivation' and 'starving for organic texture,' indicating a lack of sensory experiences. Option C is incorrect because the text contrasts the 'irregular bounty of harvests' with the 'predictable abundance of supermarkets,' not suggesting that supermarkets offer less abundance, but rather that they offer a different (predictable) kind of abundance compared to the variability of harvests.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph below and answer the question that follows.

"Community gardens have become vital resources in urban neighborhoods across the country. These shared green spaces allow residents to grow fresh produce, which improves access to healthy food in areas with limited grocery options. Beyond nutrition, community gardens foster social connections among neighbors who work together to plant and maintain the plots. Local studies show that neighborhoods with active gardens report lower crime rates and increased property values. As a result, city councils are increasingly supporting garden initiatives with funding and protected land use policies."

Which statements belong in an objective summary because they capture the central idea or the key details that develop it? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: The passage describes how community gardens benefit urban neighborhoods by improving food access, building social connections, and enhancing safety.
answer_options.B: Specific evidence, such as lower crime rates and increased property values, demonstrates the positive impact of community gardens.
answer_options.C: City councils refuse to fund community gardens because they believe they are ineffective.
answer_options.D: The author argues that all residents must participate in gardening to reduce crime.

answer_explanation: Option A correctly identifies the central idea that community gardens provide multiple benefits to urban neighborhoods, including nutritional, social, and safety improvements. Option B accurately cites specific details—lower crime rates and increased property values—that develop and support the central idea by showing measurable impacts. Option C is incorrect because it directly contradicts the passage, which states that city councils are increasingly supporting garden initiatives with funding, not refusing to fund them. Option D is incorrect because the passage does not state that all residents must participate or mandate gardening; this introduces an absolute requirement and subjective argument not present in the objective summary.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph below and answer the question that follows.

"In the twenty-first century, public libraries have undergone a fundamental transformation. No longer merely repositories for printed books, they now serve as vital community technology hubs where residents access high-speed internet, attend digital literacy workshops, and collaborate in makerspaces equipped with 3D printers. While critics argue that these changes divert resources from traditional literature collections, data from the American Library Association indicates that library visitation has actually increased by 15% since 2010. Furthermore, partnerships with local schools allow students without home internet to complete homework assignments after hours. This evolution reveals that libraries remain essential public institutions, though their role has shifted from silent sanctuaries of reading to active centers of digital inclusion and lifelong learning."

Which statements correctly identify the central idea of the paragraph AND explain how that idea is developed? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The paragraph’s central idea is that public libraries have transformed into community technology hubs, developed through examples of new services such as makerspaces and statistics showing increased visitation.
answer_options.B: The main point is that printed books are becoming obsolete in modern libraries, supported by data showing decreased book circulation and fewer visitors.
answer_options.C: The text argues that libraries remain essential public institutions despite changing functions, demonstrated by descriptions of digital services and partnerships with schools.
answer_options.D: The paragraph suggests that critics are correct about diverting resources from literature, evidenced by statistics on declining traditional book loans and empty reading rooms.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it accurately identifies the central idea—libraries have become technology hubs—and correctly cites specific details (makerspaces, workshops, the 15% visitation increase) that develop this idea. Option C is also correct; it captures the broader central argument that libraries remain essential despite their evolution, and it correctly identifies how the author uses descriptions of digital services and school partnerships to support this claim. Option B is incorrect because the text does not claim books are obsolete; in fact, it states that visitation has increased and mentions critics' concerns only to contrast them with the data. Option D is incorrect because the paragraph does not endorse the critics' view; instead, it counters their argument with data showing increased visitation and describes services that supplement rather than replace traditional functions.
subject: ELA

question: When analyzing how an author unfolds a series of ideas or events in an informational text, which of the following should you examine? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The sequence in which the author presents the main points
answer_options.B: The connections the author makes between different ideas or events
answer_options.C: How each idea is first introduced and then elaborated with details
answer_options.D: The emotional connotations of descriptive words the author uses

answer_explanation: A is correct because analyzing the order in which points are made helps reveal how the author structures the progression of ideas. B is correct because identifying the connections drawn between ideas shows how the author links concepts together to build understanding. C is correct because examining how ideas are introduced and developed reveals the author's method of presenting and expanding upon key points. D is incorrect because emotional connotations relate to word choice and tone rather than the structural unfolding of ideas, their sequence, or their connections.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage below, then answer the question that follows.

Last spring, Maria Chen noticed something troubling in her apiary outside Portland: thousands of bees lay dead beneath the hives, victims of a mysterious colony failure. Her experience mirrors a national crisis. Over the past decade, commercial beekeepers have reported annual colony losses averaging 40%, threatening both agricultural productivity and ecosystem stability. Researchers have identified three primary drivers behind this decline. First, neonicotinoid pesticides impair bees' navigation and foraging abilities. Second, monoculture farming eliminates the diverse pollen sources colonies require for nutrition. Third, rising temperatures disrupt the synchronized timing between bee emergence and flower blooming. These factors do not operate in isolation. Pesticide exposure weakens immune systems, making bees more vulnerable to nutritional stress from habitat loss, while climate change amplifies both the toxicity of certain chemicals and the geographic spread of parasites. Only by addressing these interdependencies through integrated approaches—reducing chemical inputs while restoring habitat corridors—can beekeepers like Chen hope to reverse the devastating losses witnessed in apiaries nationwide.

Which statements accurately analyze how the author unfolds the analysis of pollinator decline? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: By opening with a specific beekeeper's experience to establish a concrete foundation before expanding to national statistics and general trends
answer_options.B: By presenting the three causes of decline in the order they historically emerged, from the origins of industrial agriculture to recent climate shifts
answer_options.C: By introducing individual stress factors separately and then drawing connections to show how they compound one another
answer_options.D: By concluding with proposed solutions that synthesize the interconnected causes, linking the final practical response to the earlier analytical framework

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the author begins with Maria Chen's specific observation (concrete) before transitioning to the 'national crisis' and statistical trends (general), demonstrating a specific-to-abstract organizational pattern. Option C is correct because the author first lists the three factors as distinct entities (pesticides, habitat loss, climate change) and then develops the analysis by explaining their interactions and compounding effects, thereby drawing explicit connections between the ideas. Option D is correct because the conclusion explicitly references 'interdependencies' and proposes 'integrated approaches' that respond directly to the interconnected analytical framework established earlier, connecting the final solution back to the developed analysis. Option B is incorrect because the passage uses 'First, Second, Third' to enumerate simultaneous contemporary stressors, not to indicate a chronological historical progression from the distant past to the present.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the following sentence from a travel blog:

"The narrow mountain road was a ribbon of black asphalt, winding through the misty valleys and clinging to the cliffside."

Which statements accurately describe the figurative and connotative meanings of the phrase "a ribbon of black asphalt"? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: It literally means the road was made of silk ribbon material.
answer_options.B: It suggests the road is thin, winding, and gracefully curved.
answer_options.C: It creates a tone of elegance and delicate beauty.
answer_options.D: It uses technical engineering terminology to describe the pavement composition.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the metaphor comparing the road to a 'ribbon' figuratively conveys that the pavement is narrow, winding, and follows a graceful, curved path through the mountains. Option C is correct because 'ribbon' carries connotations of delicacy and elegance, which creates a tone that emphasizes the scenic beauty of the road rather than its danger or utility. Option A is incorrect because it interprets the phrase literally rather than as a figure of speech; the road is actually made of asphalt, not fabric. Option D is incorrect because 'ribbon' is descriptive, figurative language, not specialized technical jargon used by engineers to describe road materials.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the two excerpts below about the same public health issue, then answer the question that follows.

Excerpt 1 (Epidemiological Report): "The pathogen demonstrated **high transmissibility** and **significant morbidity rates** among the study **cohort**."

Excerpt 2 (News Blog): "The **deadly bug** **spread like wildfire**, **striking down** dozens in the neighborhood."

Which statements accurately explain how specific word choices affect meaning and tone in the two excerpts? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: "High transmissibility" and "significant morbidity" use technical terminology to convey precise data, creating an objective, clinical tone.
answer_options.B: "Deadly bug" and "spread like wildfire" employ figurative language to heighten urgency, creating a sensational tone.
answer_options.C: "Striking down" carries a violent connotation that emphasizes victimhood, whereas "cohort" maintains a neutral, scientific distance.
answer_options.D: Both excerpts use exclusively literal language to describe biological mechanisms without any tonal variation.

answer_explanation: A is correct because "high transmissibility" and "significant morbidity" are technical, precise terms typical of scientific writing, which establishes an objective, detached tone. B is correct because "deadly bug" (dysphemism/informal) and "spread like wildfire" (simile) are figurative and emotionally charged, creating urgency and sensationalism. C is correct because "striking down" connotes violence and helplessness, contrasting sharply with the neutral, collective noun "cohort," which distances the reader from individual suffering. D is incorrect because the news blog uses figurative language ("wildfire"), and the tones are distinctly different (clinical vs. sensational).
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph below.

(1) Reading fiction regularly enhances empathy and emotional intelligence in young adults. (2) Neuroscience research indicates that engaging with complex literary characters activates brain regions associated with understanding others' emotions. (3) However, the benefits depend heavily on the quality of the writing; formulaic stories without psychological depth produce minimal empathetic growth. (4) Many public libraries host book clubs for young adults to build community.

Which sentences develop or refine the author’s claim that reading fiction enhances empathy? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Sentence 1
answer_options.B: Sentence 2
answer_options.C: Sentence 3
answer_options.D: Sentence 4

answer_explanation: Sentence 2 develops the claim by providing specific scientific evidence (neuroscience research) that explains the mechanism by which reading fiction enhances empathy. Sentence 3 refines the claim by qualifying it—indicating that only high-quality fiction with psychological depth yields benefits, which tempers the initial broad assertion. Sentence 1 is incorrect because it merely states the main claim without providing development or refinement. Sentence 4 is incorrect because it provides irrelevant background information about library programs that does not address the psychological effects of reading on empathy.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the excerpt below and answer the question that follows.

(1) Urban green spaces, such as pocket parks and community gardens, significantly improve mental health outcomes for city residents. (2) Some city planners hesitate to allocate limited municipal budgets to green initiatives when infrastructure repairs demand immediate attention. (3) Yet a 2023 study published in the Journal of Environmental Psychology found that residents living within a ten-minute walk of green space reported 40 percent lower stress levels than those in concrete-heavy neighborhoods. (4) Additionally, these spaces serve as natural gathering points that strengthen social cohesion and reduce isolation among elderly residents. (5) Although the benefits are clear, cities must prioritize maintenance of existing parks before developing new ones to ensure safety and accessibility.

Which sentences develop or refine the author’s central claim that urban green spaces benefit city residents? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Sentence 2, because it introduces a competing priority that the author acknowledges to demonstrate the complexity of urban planning.
answer_options.B: Sentence 3, because it provides statistical evidence linking proximity to green space with reduced stress levels.
answer_options.C: Sentence 4, because it refines the claim by limiting the benefits to elderly residents only.
answer_options.D: Sentence 5, because it qualifies the central claim by specifying a necessary condition for maximizing benefits.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Sentence 2 introduces budgetary concerns and competing infrastructure priorities, serving as a counterargument that refines the claim by acknowledging implementation obstacles before the author addresses them with evidence. Option B is correct because Sentence 3 develops the claim by offering specific scientific data (40 percent lower stress) that substantiates the mental health benefits asserted in Sentence 1. Option D is correct because Sentence 5 refines the claim by tempering it with a practical qualification—that existing parks must be maintained before new ones are developed—thereby adding nuance to the argument. Option C is incorrect because Sentence 4 does not limit benefits to elderly residents only; rather, it expands the claim by identifying broader social cohesion benefits for the community while specifically mentioning elderly residents as one key beneficiary group.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the following excerpt from an editorial and answer the question.

Excerpt:
"Beginning high school at 7:30 AM is not merely inconvenient; it is actively harmful to adolescent development. According to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, teenagers require 8 to 10 hours of sleep for optimal brain function, yet our current schedule forces students to wake during critical REM cycles. When school districts prioritize bus logistics over student biology, they sacrifice our children's health and academic potential for mere administrative convenience. Our community must join the nationwide movement to implement 8:30 AM start times and put student wellbeing first."

Which statements correctly identify the author’s point of view or a rhetorical strategy used to advance that point of view? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The author’s primary purpose is to persuade the school district to adopt later start times to protect student health.
answer_options.B: The author cites a reputable health organization to provide scientific evidence supporting the argument.
answer_options.C: The author uses emotionally charged language such as 'actively harmful' and 'sacrifice' to appeal to the reader’s concern for children.
answer_options.D: The author presents an objective comparison of early and late start times without advocating for either schedule.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the author explicitly calls for the community to 'join the nationwide movement to implement 8:30 AM start times,' demonstrating a persuasive purpose to change the current schedule. Option B is correct because referencing the CDC establishes credibility (ethos) and uses scientific data (logos) to support the claim that teenagers need more sleep. Option C is correct because phrases like 'actively harmful,' 'sacrifice,' and 'children's health' are designed to evoke an emotional response and concern for student welfare (pathos). Option D is incorrect because the author clearly takes a stance against early start times and advocates for later ones, rather than maintaining neutrality.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the excerpt below, then answer the question that follows.

"While administrators cite bus scheduling as the reason for 7:30 AM classes, they ignore the biology staring them in the face. Adolescents are not lazy; they are sleep-deprived prisoners of circadian rhythms that science has mapped for decades. When we force teenagers to function at hours their brains treat as midnight, we aren't preparing them for the 'real world'—we're preparing them for caffeine addiction and chronic exhaustion. The research is unequivocal: later start times improve mental health, academic performance, and even traffic safety. If we truly value education, we must stop treating sleep as a luxury and start treating it as the biological necessity it is."

Which statements accurately analyze the author’s point of view or purpose and how rhetoric is used to advance it? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: The author's purpose is to persuade readers that school start times should be moved later, and the metaphor describing adolescents as 'sleep-deprived prisoners' appeals to emotion by emphasizing their lack of autonomy.
answer_options.B: The author uses specific statistical data from traffic safety studies to prove that early start times cause more accidents than late start times.
answer_options.C: The antithesis between 'aren't preparing them for the 'real world'' and 'we're preparing them for caffeine addiction' highlights the gap between intended and actual outcomes, strengthening the argument against early start times.
answer_options.D: The author maintains an objective, informative tone by presenting both the administrative justification for early start times and the scientific counterarguments without taking a definitive position.

answer_explanation: A is correct because the author explicitly argues for moving start times later (stating we 'must stop treating sleep as a luxury') and the metaphor of 'sleep-deprived prisoners' functions as pathos by framing students as victims of unjust constraints rather than lazy individuals. C is correct because the parallel grammatical structure contrasting preparation for the 'real world' versus preparation for 'caffeine addiction' creates antithesis that underscores the counterproductive nature of current policies, thereby reinforcing the persuasive claim. B is incorrect because although the author mentions traffic safety as a benefit of later start times, the excerpt does not cite specific statistical data, percentages, or studies; the argument relies on general appeals to scientific consensus rather than concrete figures. D is incorrect because the author clearly takes a definitive stance against early start times, using evaluative language such as 'ignore the biology' and 'prisoners,' which signals persuasive intent rather than objective neutrality.
subject: ELA

question: After reading a print magazine profile of youth activist Jordan Blake and watching a documentary segment about the same person, which observations show that you are analyzing how each medium emphasizes different details? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Noticing that the article lists specific statistics about policy changes, while the documentary features close-up footage of Jordan speaking at protests.
answer_options.B: Measuring the word count of the article and the exact minute length of the documentary segment.
answer_options.C: Observing that the documentary includes interviews with Jordan's family members, whereas the article emphasizes Jordan's public speeches to city council.
answer_options.D: Recording the publication date of the magazine and the production year of the documentary.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it identifies a specific difference in content emphasis: statistical data in print versus visual/emotional elements in video. Option C is correct because it contrasts the documentary's focus on personal perspectives with the article's focus on formal public actions, demonstrating analysis of how each medium highlights different aspects of the subject. Option B is incorrect because comparing length or word count addresses format rather than which details are emphasized in the content. Option D is incorrect because noting publication dates is a metadata task that does not reveal what information each source chooses to highlight about Jordan.
subject: ELA

question:
A student examines two accounts of the 1965 Selma to Montgomery civil rights marches:

Source A: On March 25, 1965, approximately 25,000 demonstrators completed the 54-mile journey from Selma to the Alabama State Capitol. Federal marshals monitored the route, and the event concluded with a rally where Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. addressed the crowd. Statistical reports indicate that 3,200 participants traveled from out of state to join the final leg of the march.

Source B: [Visual: Close-up of marchers' dusty boots on asphalt. Sound: Rhythmic thudding of feet and distant singing of 'This Little Light of Mine.'] Narrator: 'The sun beat down relentlessly for five days.' [Visual: Sweat-stained faces; an elderly woman wipes her forehead.] Interviewee: 'My feet blistered, but my spirit wouldn't break when I saw the Capitol dome.' [Sound: Roaring crowd cheering.]

Which statements accurately describe how the two accounts emphasize different details based on their mediums? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Source A emphasizes logistical details and statistical data, which reflects the informative purpose of a contemporary news report.
answer_options.B: Source B emphasizes sensory experiences and personal testimony, utilizing audio-visual elements to convey emotional immediacy.
answer_options.C: Both accounts focus primarily on providing technical specifications about the federal marshals' equipment and uniforms.
answer_options.D: Source A employs camera angles and ambient sound design to dramatize the physical hardship of the march.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Source A provides specific dates, mileage figures, and participant statistics typical of print journalism's emphasis on factual recording. Option B is correct because Source B includes stage directions for visuals and sounds ([Visual:], [Sound:]) and personal quotes that highlight emotional and sensory aspects typical of documentary film. Option C is incorrect because neither account focuses on technical equipment specifications; Source A mentions marshals only in passing as monitors, and Source B does not mention uniforms or equipment. Option D is incorrect because Source A is a text-based news report without camera angles or sound design; these are features of Source B (the documentary).
subject: ELA

question:
Read the argument below, then answer the question that follows.

"Our town should install more streetlights because my neighbor says she feels safer walking at night when lights are on. Therefore, crime rates will drop significantly once we add lights everywhere."

Which statements correctly evaluate the reasoning and evidence in this argument? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The argument uses only one person's opinion, which is insufficient evidence for a town-wide policy.
answer_options.B: The reasoning assumes that streetlights cause lower crime rates without providing data to prove this connection.
answer_options.C: The neighbor's feeling of safety is relevant and sufficient evidence to support the claim about crime rates dropping.
answer_options.D: The argument contains valid reasoning because personal testimony is always reliable evidence for public safety decisions.

answer_explanation: A is correct because the argument relies on a single anecdotal opinion rather than representative data, making the evidence insufficient for a broad policy claim. B is correct because the argument assumes a causal relationship between streetlights and crime reduction without providing statistical or logical proof, which is fallacious reasoning. C is incorrect because while personal feelings may be relevant to individual experience, they are not sufficient evidence to support a claim about objective crime statistics across an entire town. D is incorrect because personal testimony is not always reliable for broad public safety decisions, and the argument's reasoning is invalid due to its reliance on a single unverified assumption.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the argument excerpt below, then answer the question.

"All public high schools should require 100 hours of community service for graduation to build compassionate citizens. A 2022 study of 200 students at a private academy found that those who completed 20 hours of monthly volunteering reported higher empathy scores. Therefore, implementing this requirement in every public school district will definitely produce more caring citizens. Furthermore, students who oppose this requirement are simply selfish individuals avoiding responsibility, so their objections should be ignored. Since compassion is essential for democracy, any program that increases compassion must be mandated by law."

Which statements accurately evaluate the author’s reasoning and use of evidence? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Using a study of 200 private academy students with 20 monthly hours to justify a 100-hour graduation requirement for all public schools is a hasty generalization, so the reasoning is invalid.
answer_options.B: Dismissing opponents as selfish individuals who avoid responsibility is an ad hominem attack that targets the person rather than the argument, making the reasoning fallacious.
answer_options.C: The 2022 study provides irrelevant evidence because it was conducted at a private academy rather than a public school.
answer_options.D: Concluding that a program must be legally mandated solely because it promotes a desirable quality represents invalid reasoning, as the desirability of an outcome does not automatically justify its legal enforcement.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the author applies findings from a small, specific population (200 private academy students) to all public school districts and scales up the hours requirement without justification, committing a hasty generalization. Option B is correct because attacking opponents' character (calling them selfish) rather than addressing their arguments is an ad hominem fallacy. Option D is correct because the author jumps from 'compassion is good' to 'this program must be legally mandated' without establishing that legal mandate is the necessary or appropriate conclusion, which is a non sequitur or invalid logical leap. Option C is incorrect because the study about empathy is actually relevant to the claim about building compassionate citizens; the problem is not relevance but rather insufficient representativeness and questionable application (generalization).
subject: ELA

question:
Read the excerpts below from two seminal U.S. documents.

**Excerpt A (The Gettysburg Address, Abraham Lincoln):** "Four score and seven years ago our fathers brought forth on this continent, a new nation, conceived in Liberty, and dedicated to the proposition that all men are created equal."

**Excerpt B (Letter from Birmingham Jail, Martin Luther King Jr.):** "We are caught in an inescapable network of mutuality, tied in a single garment of destiny. Whatever affects one directly, affects all indirectly. Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere."

Which themes or concepts are addressed in BOTH excerpts? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: The fundamental ideal that all people possess equal rights and dignity
answer_options.B: The interconnected nature of society and shared destiny
answer_options.C: The urgency of acting immediately to correct moral wrongs
answer_options.D: The importance of protecting liberty and justice for all citizens

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Lincoln explicitly states the nation is "dedicated to the proposition that all men are created equal," while King argues that "injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere," a concept founded on the belief in equal rights for all. Option D is correct because Lincoln emphasizes the nation being "conceived in Liberty" and dedicated to equality, while King stresses the protection of justice for all citizens through his argument about the universal threat of injustice. Option B is incorrect because, while King explicitly mentions the "network of mutuality," Lincoln’s excerpt focuses on the nation’s founding political ideals rather than the interconnectedness of individuals. Option C is incorrect because King elsewhere emphasizes immediate action (e.g., "justice too long delayed is justice denied"), but Lincoln’s speech focuses on commemorating sacrifice and reaffirming enduring principles rather than urgent corrective action.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the excerpts below from two seminal U.S. documents. Then select all statements that accurately analyze how the authors address related themes of national identity and justice.

**Excerpt A (Abraham Lincoln, *Gettysburg Address*, 1863):** "Four score and seven years ago our fathers brought forth on this continent, a new nation, conceived in Liberty, and dedicated to the proposition that all men are created equal. Now we are engaged in a great civil war, testing whether that nation, or any nation so conceived and so dedicated, can long endure."

**Excerpt B (Martin Luther King Jr., *Letter from Birmingham Jail*, 1963):** "Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere. We are caught in an inescapable network of mutuality, tied in a single garment of destiny. Whatever affects one directly, affects all indirectly."

answer_options.A: Both excerpts argue that the nation's founding commitment to equality and liberty is currently being tested by a profound moral crisis.
answer_options.B: Both texts assert that the denial of rights to any group threatens the stability and moral integrity of the entire society.
answer_options.C: Both authors employ anaphora, the repetition of a word or phrase at the beginning of successive sentences, to create a sense of urgency.
answer_options.D: Both excerpts explicitly demand the immediate passage of federal legislation to abolish slavery and racial segregation, respectively.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Lincoln explicitly states that the Civil War is "testing whether that nation... can long endure," framing the conflict as a test of the nation's dedication to its founding proposition. Similarly, King's assertion that "injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere" frames the struggle against segregation as a test of the nation's moral and civic health. Option B is correct because King explicitly describes an "inescapable network of mutuality, tied in a single garment of destiny" to argue that individual and collective fates are inseparable, while Lincoln implies that the nation's survival depends on its adherence to the principle that "all men are created equal," meaning that systemic inequality threatens national collapse. Option C is incorrect because neither excerpt uses anaphora (repetition at the beginning of successive sentences); Lincoln uses periodic structure, and King uses balanced parallel phrases. Option D is incorrect because while King calls for moral and legal action, he does not explicitly demand specific federal legislation in this excerpt, and Lincoln’s address commemorates the war dead and dedicates the nation to the unfinished work of equality rather than explicitly demanding the passage of a constitutional amendment to abolish slavery.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the excerpt and answer the question.

"Maya clutched the acceptance letter, her eyes scanning the words for the fifth time. A grin spread across her face as she jumped up from the kitchen chair. \"I got in!\" she shouted, waving the paper at her mother. The coffee mug sat forgotten on the table, steam still rising."

Which details from the excerpt provide strong textual evidence that Maya is excited about the acceptance letter? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: "Maya clutched the acceptance letter"
answer_options.B: "A grin spread across her face"
answer_options.C: "I got in!\" she shouted
answer_options.D: "The coffee mug sat forgotten on the table"

answer_explanation: Option B provides strong explicit evidence because a grin is a direct physical expression of happiness and excitement. Option C provides strong explicit evidence because shouting \"I got in!\" is a direct verbal expression of joy. Option D provides strong inferential evidence because the forgotten coffee mug indicates Maya is too distracted by her excitement to notice her surroundings. Option A is not strong evidence because clutching the letter could indicate anxiety, tension, or disbelief rather than specifically excitement, making it ambiguous for this claim.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the excerpt below, then answer the question.

"Rosa stared at the acceptance letter, her thumbnail catching on the envelope's torn edge. She traced the embossed seal slowly, then folded the paper twice and slid it into her desk drawer, pressing down until the latch clicked shut. 'Later,' she whispered to the empty room, though her shoulders had already begun to curve inward like a wilting stem."

Which of the following details from the passage provide strong textual evidence to support the inference that Rosa is experiencing anxiety or hesitation about her acceptance? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: "her thumbnail catching on the envelope's torn edge"
answer_options.B: "She traced the embossed seal slowly"
answer_options.C: "folded the paper twice and slid it into her desk drawer"
answer_options.D: "her shoulders had already begun to curve inward like a wilting stem"

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the detail about her thumbnail catching on the torn edge suggests nervous fidgeting, which physically manifests anxiety. Option C is correct because folding the letter and hiding it in a drawer demonstrates avoidance behavior, indicating hesitation about confronting the news. Option D is correct because the simile comparing her posture to a wilting stem conveys a physical burden or emotional weight, supporting the inference of anxiety. Option B is incorrect because tracing the seal slowly could indicate careful reading or admiration of the achievement, but it does not specifically signal anxiety or hesitation.
subject: ELA

question: Which of the following scenarios demonstrate behaviors that satisfy the expectations of CCSS.ELA-LITERACY.RL.9-10.10? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: A student independently reads a short story within the grades 9–10 text complexity band and writes a clear analysis of the central conflict.
answer_options.B: A student comprehends a drama at the high end of the grades 9–10 text complexity band while using a teacher-provided graphic organizer and vocabulary list.
answer_options.C: A student struggles to follow the plot of a grades 9–10 band novel and submits incomplete reading notes showing minimal understanding.
answer_options.D: A student reads a folk tale written for middle school audiences and summarizes the moral with teacher assistance.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it demonstrates independent, proficient comprehension of literature within the grades 9–10 text complexity band, meeting the expectations for both grade 9 (within the band) and grade 10 (independence). Option B is correct because it shows proficient comprehension of a high-end band text with appropriate scaffolding, which specifically aligns with the grade 9 expectation that allows support at the high end of the range. Option C is incorrect because the student fails to comprehend the text proficiently, violating the standard's core requirement. Option D is incorrect because the text is below the grades 9–10 complexity band; comprehending it does not demonstrate the grade-level reading proficiency required by the standard.
subject: ELA

question: A teacher assigns a complex literary text from the high end of the grades 9–10 text complexity band to her class. Which of the following scenarios demonstrate that students are meeting the expectations of CCSS.ELA-LITERACY.RL.9-10.10 for reading and comprehending such literature? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Students independently read the assigned text and analyze the author's use of literary devices to develop themes, requiring no additional support to understand the narrative structure.
answer_options.B: Students read the text with occasional teacher-provided glossaries for archaic vocabulary and historical context, then demonstrate comprehension by analyzing character motivations and thematic elements.
answer_options.C: Students read a simplified adaptation of the text written at a sixth-grade reading level to prepare for the full reading.
answer_options.D: Students are unable to identify the main conflict even after the teacher reads the text aloud and provides a summary of each chapter.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it demonstrates the Grade 10 expectation of reading and comprehending complex literature independently and proficiently at the high end of the grades 9–10 band. Option B is correct because it reflects the Grade 9 expectation of comprehending such literature proficiently with scaffolding as needed for challenging vocabulary and historical references.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the passage below. Which details help develop the theme that preserving heritage and practicing patience hold value even in a modern, efficiency-driven society? Select ALL that apply.

Lila sat on the porch steps, watching her father repair the antique bicycle that had belonged to her grandfather. The bike’s thin tires and steel frame seemed out of place in a neighborhood where electric scooters zipped past. Her father had insisted on fixing the broken chain himself, refusing Lila’s offer to buy him a new e-bike for his birthday. 'Your grandfather rode this to work every day for thirty years,' her father said, wiping grease from his hands. 'Some things deserve to be maintained, not replaced.' Lila, who had always prioritized efficiency and speed, suddenly saw the value in her father’s patient labor. She picked up the spare wrench and asked if she could help, bridging the gap between her fast-paced world and his deliberate care.

answer_options.A: The description of the antique bicycle’s thin tires and steel frame contrasting with the modern electric scooters.
answer_options.B: Lila’s offer to purchase a new e-bike for her father’s birthday.
